mnemonics: Dummy variables are like FACTORY LABELS: they assign a category a numerical code (0/1) to make it usable in regression models, just as a factory labels products for identification.
keyPoints: Factor variables represent categorical data and must be converted into a numerical format for regression models.
Dummy coding (one-hot encoding) is the most common technique, creating k-1 new variables for a factor with k levels to avoid the dummy variable trap.
The coefficients for dummy variables represent the difference in the expected outcome between that level and the reference level, holding other variables constant.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set a reference category, leading to perfect multicollinearity (the dummy variable trap).
Interpreting the coefficient for a dummy variable as the absolute effect of that category rather than the effect relative to the reference category.
